text:
Question: How does backpropagation compute gradients in neural networks?

Answer: Backpropagation applies the chain rule layer by layer. Each weight receives a gradient proportional to the partial derivative of the loss with respect to that weight.

Additional Answer: In practice, frameworks like PyTorch build a computation graph and call backward() to populate .grad tensors.

Comment: +1 great explanation
Comment: This is wrong
actual_output:
Backpropagation applies the chain rule layer by layer. Each weight receives a gradient proportional to the partial derivative of the loss with respect to that weight.
Additional Answer: In practice, frameworks like PyTorch build a computation graph and call backward() to populate .grad tensors.

text:
Why does my Python list comprehension run out of memory on large datasets?

I tried using range(10**8) and my 16GB RAM fills up instantly.

Top comment (score 847): Use a generator expression instead: (x for x in range(n)). Generators yield one item at a time without building the full list in memory.

Reply (score 12): Or use numpy arrays for numeric data.

Edited 2 hours ago | Share | Save | Report
expected_keep: yield one item at a time
actual_output:
Why does my Python list comprehension run out of memory on large datasets?
I tried using range(10**8) and my 16GB RAM fills up instantly.
Top comment (score 847): Use a generator expression instead: (x for x in range(n)). Generators yield one item at a time without building the full list in memory.

text:
Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What is overfitting?
A: Overfitting occurs when a model learns training data noise instead of generalizable patterns, performing well on training but poorly on unseen data.

Q: How do I detect overfitting?
A: Monitor validation loss divergence from training loss and use cross-validation or regularization techniques.

Comments: helpful thanks
actual_output:
Monitor validation loss divergence from training loss and use cross-validation or regularization techniques.
Overfitting occurs when a model learns training data noise instead of generalizable patterns, performing well on training but poorly on unseen data.
